data.title: Credit Clear: UK Acquisitions Expand Market Reach and Earnings Upside, Supporting Maintained Buy Rating
data.description:
Credit Clear Limited, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Caleb Weng from PAC Partners maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a A$0.45 price target.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Caleb Weng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to Credit Clear’s recent UK acquisitions and resulting growth outlook. He believes the purchases of ARC and DTS significantly broaden the company’s addressable market, particularly in the UK, while adding a complementary, digital-only collections capability focused on automated voice engagement. On his revised forecasts, the incremental contribution from these businesses lifts FY26 and FY27 EBITDA, even before factoring in any revenue synergies from cross-selling or platform rollout, which he views as a source of potential upside. In addition, both acquisitions bring established relationships with blue‑chip clients in financial services, telecoms, utilities and government, creating fertile ground for upselling CCR’s broader solution suite.

Weng also highlights that the company’s valuation remains attractive relative to its earnings profile, with CCR trading on what he considers a low FY27 EV/EBITDA multiple of around 5x. He notes that the integration plan includes some one‑off costs but also the prospect of cost efficiencies over time, such as consolidating ARC and DTS support functions into a single UK office. The ability to monetize DTS’s new voice‑based channel across CCR’s existing customer base, combined with the expanded UK presence, is expected to underpin strong medium‑term earnings growth. Taken together—expanding market reach, complementary technology, cross‑sell and cost synergy potential, and a modest valuation—these dynamics support his maintained Buy recommendation and $0.45 per share target price.

According to TipRanks, Weng is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 27.1% and a 65.38% success rate. Weng covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Raiz Invest Ltd., EPX, and Credit Clear Limited.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
